audit_metadata.scope: Full codebase scan including API routes, dependencies, auth, and secrets

findings.title: Multiple API Keys and Secrets Exposed in Version Control
findings.description: The .env.local, .env.vps, and .env.sentry files contain sensitive production credentials that should never be committed to version control.
findings.impact: CRITICAL - Attackers could gain unauthorized access to: database, admin accounts, SMS/Telegram services, Google OAuth credentials, LLM APIs, payment systems, and customer data.
action_items: Rotate ALL exposed secrets immediately (API keys, tokens, database credentials)
Add .env.local and .env.vps to .gitignore if not already there
Use environment variable management system (Vercel Secrets, HashiCorp Vault, 1Password)
Remove secrets from git history: git-filter-branch or BFG Repo-Cleaner
Store secrets in Vercel Environment Variables only
Implement pre-commit hooks to prevent secret commits
Enable GitHub secret scanning

findings.title: Multiple Endpoints Use String Comparison for API Keys
findings.description: API endpoints validate API keys using simple string comparison without rate limiting or proper mechanisms.
vulnerabilities: No API key rotation or expiration
Single API key used for multiple services
No request signing or HMAC validation
action_items: Implement API key rotation with expiration dates
Use HMAC-SHA256 for key comparison
Add per-API-key rate limiting
Create separate API keys for different services/scopes
Implement request signing (HMAC of body + timestamp)
Add audit logging for all API key usage

findings.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Chat Module
findings.description: File upload endpoint constructs paths from user input without full validation.
issues: File extension not validated (relies on Content-Type check)
Fallback to local filesystem is risky
path.basename() used for safety but still allows some attacks
action_items: Validate file extensions against allowlist (jpg, png, gif, webp)
Use random UUIDs for filenames, not original names
Never store uploads in /public directory
Implement antivirus scanning for uploaded files
Remove local filesystem fallback, use Vercel Blob only
Validate Content-Type with magic bytes verification

findings.title: Admin Phone Numbers and Credentials Exposed
findings.description: Administrative contact information and credentials accessible via environment variables.
exposure_points: Embedded in SMS notifications that could be logged
Telegram bot token in environment variables
action_items: Move sensitive contact info to secure secret management
Implement structured logging that filters sensitive fields
Use correlation IDs instead of logging credentials
Configure log redaction for phone, email, token, api_key patterns
Exclude environment variables from default logging

findings.title: State-Changing Operations Lack CSRF Tokens
findings.description: API endpoints accepting x-api-key may bypass CSRF protections.
findings.recommendation: All POST/PATCH/DELETE should require CSRF tokens or secure session validation.
action_items: Verify NextAuth.js CSRF settings are enabled
Implement double-submit cookie pattern for API key endpoints
Add SameSite=Strict cookie policy
Ensure all state-changing operations require proper tokens

findings.title: Potential SQL Injection in Dynamic Queries
findings.description: While parameterized queries are used, dynamic WHERE clause construction could be vulnerable if validation fails.
findings.current_status: SAFE - Whitelist validation is properly implemented
findings.note: Document this pattern for future developers. Keep whitelist validation as best practice.

findings.title: Rate Limit Map Could Cause Memory Issues
findings.description: In-memory rate limiting with potential for unbounded growth under attack.
concerns: Could reach memory limit during botnet attack
Arcjet already integrated but only in lib/arcjet.ts
action_items: Use Arcjet for all rate limiting instead of in-memory
Implement sliding window counter algorithm
Add per-key memory limits and circuit breaker
Monitor rate limit map size and alert on growth
Consider Redis for distributed rate limiting

findings.title: PostgreSQL Password Exposed in DATABASE_URL
findings.description: Database credentials embedded in connection string accessible to all processes.
action_items: Use Neon IAM authentication (JWT-based) if available
Rotate database password immediately
Use Vercel PostgreSQL with automatic secret management
Implement separate connection pooling credentials
Create read-only user for specific application functions

findings.title: Content-Security-Policy Not Configured
findings.description: While some security headers are set, CSP is missing which prevents XSS attacks.
action_items: Add Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Add X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none
Add Permissions-Policy restrictions

findings.title: User Input Truncated Without Validation
findings.description: Input strings are silently truncated using .slice() without error feedback.
risks: User doesn't know data was truncated
action_items: Implement proper validation using zod or yup
Return validation errors instead of silently truncating
Set maximum length constraints at API boundary
Validate before database insertion

findings.title: No Automated Dependency Scanning in CI/CD
findings.description: No visible npm audit or Snyk integration in GitHub Actions.
action_items: Add npm audit to pre-commit hooks
Configure Snyk in GitHub Actions
Set up Dependabot for automated pull requests
Run security audits in CI/CD pipeline on every push

findings.title: Error Messages Could Leak System Details
findings.description: Generic error messages in production (good) but could reveal patterns.
findings.current_status: ACCEPTABLE - Generic errors are properly implemented
recommendations: Log detailed errors server-side with correlation IDs
Return only correlation ID for debugging

compliance_gaps.gdpr: INCOMPLETE - Data collection present (email, phone) but missing: data retention policy, right to deletion endpoint, data export functionality
compliance_gaps.pci_dss: NOT_APPLICABLE - No direct credit card processing (uses Interac/bank transfers)
compliance_gaps.soc2: PARTIAL - Rate limiting and monitoring in place, audit logging needs enhancement

priority_fixes.item: Rotate all secrets in .env files immediately
Remove .env files from git history (BFG Repo-Cleaner)
priority_fixes.blockers: Need to coordinate with team on force-push

testing_plan: Run: npm audit and fix all warnings
Run: snyk test to check known vulnerabilities
Test: API endpoints with invalid/missing API keys
Test: File upload with various filename patterns
Test: Rate limiting with ab (Apache Bench) or similar
Test: CSRF protection on state-changing operations
Penetration test with Burp Suite Community Edition

immediate: Use git-filter-branch to remove secrets from history
Add comprehensive .gitignore for all sensitive files
short_term_1week: Implement HMAC validation for API keys
Add CSP and other security headers
medium_term_1month: Implement API key expiration and rotation
